title: Cirrus: Use imgts container to record metadata (needs #2213)
body:
Make use of the built imgts container image to track
VM image usage statistics for every automation run.

***. DEPENDS ON #2213 .***

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@baude @mheon This is a followup to #2213 and also depends on ``quay.io/libpod/imgts:latest`` being built: *Until then, the 'meta' test (here) will fail.*

However I did [test the workflow using an image from my registry.](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6249203488522240).  I can see the updated metadata (labels):

```
[cevich@cevich libpod]$ pgcloud --configuration=libpod compute images describe fedora-28-libpod-9afa57a9 | grep -a3 'labels'
id: '3811958042047208175'
kind: compute#image
labelFingerprint: nD4kG5Ay78k=
labels:
  build-id: '5524526675787776'
  last-used: '1548345695'
  repo-ref: 38d305263720ad9bd964eb690eccf77ad6ec5a44
```

The new 'meta' task can handle some [failures as well, such as a missing or bad image name.](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5979181209878528)  Exiting successfully despite one failed update.

We don't want random joe-schmoe modifying or clobbering our VM images to do evil things.  So to prove I [got the permissions correct, I tried adding a command to delete an image.](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6193330527404032)  Updates were successful, and the image-delete failed as expected.

Lastly, I added a small validation check to the gating job.  This will make sure the list of images used for testing are also always labeled with metadata.  I tested the new gating image in the same way and it seems to run cleanly.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2206) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
(rebase & force-push)
/retest
/retest
/retest
/retest

[Meta task successful:](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6301500369272832)

```
/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh
Updated property [core/account].

Activated service account credentials for: [SECRET@SECRET.iam.gserviceaccount.com]
Updating labels of image [fedora-28-libpod-7f4cd1f7]...
Updating labels of image [rhel-7-libpod-7f4cd1f7]...
Updating labels of image [ubuntu-18-libpod-7f4cd1f7]...
Updating labels of image [fedora-29-libpod-7f4cd1f7]...
Updating labels of image [image-builder-image-1541772081]...
.....done.
done.
done.
done.
done.
```
Since #2213 is merged, are we ready to merge this?
@rhatdan yes, good to go (IMHO).  I manually confirmed the CI run actually updated metadata (on the VM images in our cloud).

LGTM
@edsantiago @baude PTAL
**note to self**: I should rebase and re-run the tests here to confirm they're all still working.

I've skimmed this a few times now but don't feel qualified to lgtm or even comment: I don't have the necessary Cirrus knowledge.
@edsantiago None of us other then @cevich do.  :^)

/approve
I think we can merge after v1.1
